data.title: Spot silver prices have climbed above $100 per ounce, entering the "triple-digit era".
data.description: APMEX (American Precious Metals Exchange) quotes show that spot silver prices once rose above $100/ounce, but have now fallen back to $99.71/ounce; quotes from other major brokerage platforms show that spot silver has not yet broken through the $100 mark and is currently around $99. (Jinshi)

data.title: Cathie Wood: Quantum computing may not be truly commercialized until the mid-to-late 2040s
data.description: Cathie Wood, founder of ARK Invest, wrote, “One of the most frequent questions we receive about innovation and future trends is about quantum computing. We’ve done a lot of research on the current stage of quantum computing. We believe that commercial applications related to quantum computing will likely emerge in the mid-to-late 2040s, roughly between the mid-to-late 2040s and the mid-2060s. Throughout my career, the real commercial application of quantum computing has always seemed like ‘20 years from now.’ And I’ve been in this field for quite some time. So right now, perhaps we’re still 19 years away from real commercial applications.”

data.title: Analysis: Widening US Treasury yield spreads reduce the attractiveness of high-beta assets, making it more difficult for Bitcoin to return to $100,000.
data.description: Data shows that the spread in US Treasury yields has widened to its highest level since 2021, indicating a growing caution in the market regarding the outlook for risk assets, including Bitcoin. David Roberts, head of fixed income at Nedgroup Investments, stated that persistently high yields will put downward pressure on global equities, with the pressure primarily concentrated on long-term Treasury bonds. Rising long-term yields increase the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding assets, thereby diminishing the attractiveness of high-beta risk assets such as stocks and Bitcoin. Furthermore, the relative strength of gold is also seen as another obstacle to Bitcoin. Bloomberg Intelligence strategist Mike McGlone stated that gold is undergoing a "historic alpha gain," attracting inflows as long-term US Treasury yields rise. If investors continue to favor low-volatility store-of-value assets, it may become more difficult for Bitcoin to return to key psychological levels such as $100,000. (CoinTelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin mining company Cango mined 115.4 BTC this week, bringing its total holdings to approximately 7,758 BTC.
data.description: Cango, a US-listed Bitcoin mining company, announced on the X platform that it mined 115.4 BTC this week, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to approximately 7,758, with a current deployed hashrate of 50 EH/s.

data.title: SPACECOIN, an address suspected to be associated with the project team, has seen continuous large-scale transfers totaling approximately 150 million SPACE tokens.
data.description: BscScan data shows that a suspected project/organization-affiliated address on the BNB Chain has recently seen continuous large-scale transfers of SPACECOIN, with a cumulative outflow of approximately 150 million SPACE tokens. This address currently still holds approximately 500 million tokens. The transfers were mostly made in batches, with destinations including multiple new addresses.

data.title: Cango Reports Weekly Bitcoin Mining Output
data.description: Cango, a publicly traded Bitcoin mining company, announced on the X platform that it produced 115.4 BTC this week. According to Odaily, the company's total Bitcoin holdings have reached approximately 7,758 BTC. The current deployed computing power stands at 50 EH/s.

data.title: Revolut Seeks U.S. Banking License Amid Global Expansion Efforts
data.description:
Revolut, a fintech unicorn known for its crypto-friendly approach, is planning to apply for a banking license in the United States, shifting away from its previous strategy of acquiring a local lender. According to Cointelegraph, this move is part of Revolut's broader ambition to expand its global footprint. The United Kingdom-based company has been in talks with U.S. officials about obtaining a bank license through the Office for the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC), as reported by the Financial Times.

If successful, this would mark a significant strategic milestone for Revolut, which had initially considered purchasing a U.S. bank to expedite its international growth. Founded in the UK, Revolut announced its global strategy in 2025, committing $13 billion over five years to support its expansion. Following a share sale in November, the company was valued at $75 billion, making it one of the world's most valuable fintech firms. Revolut's U.S. CEO, Sid Jajodia, expressed the company's ambition to establish itself as a bank in the U.S., emphasizing the importance of obtaining a banking license to scale in the market. He noted that the company was still exploring various pathways as of September 2025 and highlighted the need for a streamlined regulatory process.

Revolut's decision to pursue a U.S. banking license rather than acquiring a local lender is influenced by the expectation of a faster approval process under the Trump administration's crypto-friendly regulatory stance. Additionally, acquiring a community bank could have required Revolut to maintain physical branch networks, which the company aims to avoid. Cointelegraph reached out to Revolut for confirmation of these plans but had not received a response by the time of publication.

In the UK, Revolut has prioritized becoming a fully-fledged lender, although it has yet to roll out all banking services to its customers. The company received a restricted banking license from the UK's Prudential Regulation Authority in 2024. As part of its global expansion, Revolut has been securing regulatory approvals in various markets. In late 2025, it obtained banking licenses in Colombia and Mexico and, in October, acquired a Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation license from the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 15,112 ETH and 249.5 BTC into Coinbase.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about an hour ago, BlackRock deposited 15,112 ETH (approximately $44.38 million) and 249.5 BTC (approximately $22.25 million) into Coinbase.

data.title: ZachXBT: Hacker John flaunts $23 million in assets; may be involved in $90 million US government theft.
data.description: Chain detective ZachXBT published an article on the X platform stating that John (Lick) exposed a wallet address containing $23 million while arguing with another threat actor, Dritan Kapplani Jr., about whose crypto wallet had more funds. These funds are linked to over $90 million in suspected theft from the US government in 2024, and to multiple other cases involving unidentified victims between November and December 2025. The entire incident was recorded.

data.title: Binance Wallet App and Web version now support direct trading of Meme coins using stablecoins.
data.description: According to official sources, Binance Wallet App and Web version now support direct trading of Meme tokens using stablecoins. Users can switch payment currencies in any trading interface and directly purchase MEME tokens using stablecoins. Currently supported stablecoins include USDT, USDC, U, and USD1.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Challenges Amid Rising Japanese Bond Yields
data.description:
Delphi Digital has noted that while Bitcoin's price remains stagnant, gold continues to rise. According to Odaily, this trend may be linked to Japanese government bonds. Typically, rising yields increase the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding assets, putting pressure on gold. However, when gold and yields rise together, it indicates market pricing of policy pressure and balance sheet vulnerabilities rather than economic growth.

Currently, the yield on Japan's 10-year government bonds is about 3.65 standard deviations above the long-term average. The Bank of Japan structurally holds long-term bonds and is deeply exposed to Japanese government bonds in terms of assets and collateral.

Gold is absorbing this pressure, while Bitcoin shows a negative correlation with Japan's 10-year government bonds. Over the long term, Bitcoin has struggled as Japanese yields rise. If the Bank of Japan intervenes to stabilize the bond market, the risk premium in gold may ease, providing Bitcoin with room to rebound.

data.title: Bubblemaps: 70% of RALPH traders have exited the market; the stock plummeted 97% after developers sold off their holdings.
data.description: Blockchain analytics platform Bubblemaps reported on its X platform that RALPH has fallen 97% since the developers dumped their holdings. 70% of traders have sold, while 30% are still holding. Those who chose to hold have accumulated losses of $2 million.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin is negatively correlated with Japanese government bond yields; Bank of Japan intervention may trigger a rebound.
data.description: Delphi Digital published an article stating, "Bitcoin prices are stagnating while gold continues to rise. The reason may lie in Japanese government bonds. Typically, rising yields increase the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding assets, thus putting pressure on gold. However, when gold and yields rise in tandem, the market is actually pricing in policy pressures and balance sheet vulnerabilities, rather than economic growth. The yield on 10-year Japanese government bonds is currently about 3.65 standard deviations above its long-term average. The Bank of Japan structurally holds long-term bonds and is deeply exposed to Japanese government bonds in terms of both assets and collateral. Gold is absorbing this pressure, while Bitcoin is negatively correlated with 10-year Japanese government bonds and has struggled relatively in the longer term as Japanese yields have risen. If the Bank of Japan intervenes to stabilize the government bond market, the risk premium in gold may ease, and Bitcoin will have room to rebound."

data.title: HexTrust deposited 6,230 AAVE and $8.92 million worth of stablecoins into Binance in the past four hours.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, HexTrust deposited 6,230 AAVE (worth approximately $983,000) and $8.92 million worth of stablecoins into Binance in the past four hours.

data.title: Solana Staking Hits Record High Amid Price Decline
data.description: Solana's staking amount has reached an unprecedented $60 billion, with 70% of its tokens staked, indicating robust investor confidence. According to NS3.AI, despite this positive staking activity, the price of SOL has experienced a significant drop from its peak of $293 in January 2025 to below $130 in early 2026. The decline in SOL's price is closely linked to Bitcoin's bearish trend. However, historical patterns suggest that Solana may experience a rebound if Bitcoin's price recovers.

data.title: OpenEden, FalconX, and Monarq jointly launched the tokenized yield portfolio PRISM.
data.description: OpenEden, in a post on the X platform, announced the upcoming launch of PRISM, a tokenized yield portfolio in partnership with FalconX and Monarq. This product aims to achieve stable returns across market cycles and maintain a low correlation with cryptocurrency prices through a multi-strategy quantitative model actively managed by Monarq. PRISM's investment strategies include spot-to-futures arbitrage, over-collateralized lending to institutional counterparties, participation in on-chain yield platforms, and allocation to regulated tokenized real-world assets, including US Treasury-backed assets. Yields will be distributed through a staking mechanism, allowing users to stake PRISM to earn xPRISM, a value-accumulating certificate reflecting portfolio performance over time. The product is currently based on the Ethereum network and will gradually expand to more networks. PRISM has a limited pre-deposit activity prior to its launch.

data.title: GameStop appears to have transferred all its on-chain BTC to Coinbase Prime.
data.description: According to market sources, GameStop is suspected of transferring all its on-chain BTC to Coinbase Prime, or it may be selling them. (Cointelegraph) It is reported that between May 14th and 23rd, 2025, GameStop purchased 4,710 Bitcoins at an average price of $1.079 million, representing an investment of approximately $504 million. If sold at a price of $90,800, this could result in a loss of approximately $76 million.

data.title: Analysis: Ethereum mainnet activity has surpassed many L2 servers, but this may be related to the rise of address poisoning attacks.
data.description: Data shows that Ethereum's daily active addresses rebounded in January, briefly surpassing major Layer 2 networks, driven by lower transaction fees. According to Token Terminal, Ethereum's daily active addresses approached 1 million this month, briefly rising to approximately 1.3 million on January 16th, before falling back to around 950,000. However, analysts warn that the active address data may be overestimated. Security researcher Andrey Sergeenkov pointed out that the January address growth is highly correlated with attacks such as address poisoning, where attackers send extremely small stablecoin transfers to numerous wallets, causing fake addresses to appear in victims' transaction records, thus inducing them to transfer funds unnecessarily. Overall, Ethereum mainnet activity does show signs of recovery, but short-term data contains a large amount of non-genuine demand, and the quality of the on-chain "recovery" remains to be seen. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Regulatory Changes to Boost Bitcoin and Ether ETFs
data.description: New regulatory relaxations are poised to enhance the influence of Bitcoin and Ether ETFs by easing options trading restrictions. According to NS3.AI, these changes are anticipated to boost market participation, liquidity, and investor flexibility. The update indicates a potential expansion of crypto investment products and improved accessibility for both retail and institutional investors.

data.title: Tristan Thompson Launches Innovative Basketball Prediction Platform
data.description: Tristan Thompson, a veteran NBA player, has unveiled basketball.fun, a groundbreaking prediction market platform. According to NS3.AI, this platform enables users to trade leading basketball athletes akin to financial assets. The initiative combines sports statistics with market speculation, offering a unique approach to engaging with basketball.
